teachingPoint: Bundle activation must validate the complete bundle before any provisioning side effect starts. BundleValidator reads the manifest, verifies every required flow, and enqueues the full cross-flow BFA check before BundleActivationOrchestrator can proceed.
positiveExample: T574 validates requiredFlows and returns valid true before T575 starts DRY_RUN.
negativeExample: T575 starts provisioning the first flow while later required flows have not been validated.
discriminatingConstraint: The handoff from T574 to T575 requires a valid BundleValidationReport.
keywords: bundle validation complete requiredFlows BFA before activation

teachingPoint: Every required flow in a solution bundle must pass DRY_RUN before FULL activation. Skipping the preview removes the safety gate that protects tenants from partial provisioning.
positiveExample: T575 calls bootstrapFlow(flowId, { mode: 'DRY_RUN' }) and only then calls bootstrapFlow(flowId, { mode: 'FULL' }).
negativeExample: T575 calls FULL directly for each required flow.
discriminatingConstraint: A FULL activation call is legal only after the same flow has a successful DRY_RUN result in the current activation.
keywords: DRY_RUN before FULL required flow activation safety

teachingPoint: Bundle activation may seed FREEDOM defaults, but it must never overwrite a tenant's existing values. Activation adds missing defaults only.
positiveExample: T575 checks existing FREEDOM keys and stores only keys that are absent.
negativeExample: T575 writes the bundle default object over the tenant's current FREEDOM config.
discriminatingConstraint: Existing tenant FREEDOM keys always win over bundle defaults.
keywords: FREEDOM defaults additive existing tenant values preserved

teachingPoint: BundleStatusTracker must check all bundles that include a regenerated flow and must scope status changes to the current tenant. A degraded bundle in one tenant cannot degrade another tenant's bundle.
positiveExample: T576 finds every active bundle containing the regenerated flow, persists the tenant-scoped status transition, then emits BundleDegraded or BundleRestored.
negativeExample: T576 updates the first matching bundle globally and emits a degradation event before persistence.
discriminatingConstraint: Bundle status transition is tenant-scoped and persisted before event emission.
keywords: BundleStatusTracker all bundles tenant scoped degraded restored persisted before enqueue
